run_3 Network-Exposure Analysis

This report validates whether the empirical exposure-network layer is functioning in run 3 and summarizes the first descriptive insights from the four-state measurement backbone: private baseline (B), baseline network exposure (BN), private post-attack (P), and post-attack network exposure (PN).

Profiles assigned
100
empirical positions
BN leaf tasks
1517
0 fallbacks
PN leaf tasks
1517
0 fallbacks
Scenario rows
1517
Stage 05 output
Main result. Run 3 supports the new exposure layer mechanically and analytically. The network phases use empirical incoming exposure edges, not profile similarity; the post-attack network phase adds a positive mean direction-aware increment of 6.77 score points after private attack exposure.

1. Measurement Backbone Is Complete

The first validation question is whether every intended phase exists at the correct unit of analysis. The assignment phase operates once per profile, BN once per profile-opinion task, PN once per profile-opinion-attack condition, and Stage 05 expands the completed measurements into the scenario-level analysis table.

phase expected_unit completed fallback_or_skipped core_validity_check
01b profile-position assignment unique profile 100 0 all profiles assigned to one empirical position
02b baseline network exposure profile × opinion 1517 0 same-opinion incoming empirical peers
04b post-attack network exposure profile × opinion leaf (attacks pooled) 1517 0 same-condition incoming empirical peers
05 effect construction scenario row 1517 0 B, BN, P, PN and exposure covariates flattened

2. Peer Contexts Are Empirical Exposure Neighborhoods

The central implementation claim is that peer context now follows empirical directed exposure edges: source_position_id -> target_position_id, meaning visible peer to exposed receiver. The context payloads contain exposure_weight and no affinity field. Full incoming peer metrics are preserved, while prompt rationales are bounded to at most eight exemplars.

BN mean incoming peer count 25.932762

BN max prompt exemplars 8.000000
BN contexts with affinity string 0.000000
BN contexts with exposure_weight 1517.000000
PN contexts 1517.000000
PN mean incoming peer count 25.932762
PN min incoming peer count 1.000000
PN max incoming peer count 61.000000
PN max prompt exemplars 8.000000

3. Network Exposure Changes The Measurement State

The four-state design separates three quantities that should not be collapsed: the baseline peer-context shift (BN - B), the private attack effect ((P - B) × d), and the post-attack network increment ((PN - P) × d). In run 3, the average private attack effect is 18.94, the average post-network increment is 6.77, and the average final network-exposed effect is 25.71.

metric n mean sd min p25 median p75 max
ae_private 1517 18.940013 14.800264 0.0 13.00000 15.0000 24.00000 135.0
pn_increment_effectivity 1517 6.771259 7.314826 -12.0 3.00000 7.0000 11.00000 201.0
ae_total_network 1517 25.711272 18.257904 0.0 16.00000 21.0000 31.00000 216.0
peer_private_attack_activation 1375 -14.372113 15.371579 -94.0 -20.00225 -15.4479 -10.78925 125.0

4. Private Susceptibility Differs By Separable Attack Factor

Each scenario carries its own near-unique DISARM Plan/Prepare/Execute triplet, so a single opinion x full-triplet cell is a singleton. Instead the triplet is decomposed into SEPARABLE attack factors (the shared 2nd-level tactic node of each phase), and each factor's marginal contribution to the direction-aware effect is estimated across the many triplets. This isolates the individual contribution of the attack factor rather than confounding it with one specific operation.

5. Peer Activation Tracks Post-Attack Network Amplification

H2 is the most direct validation of the network layer: if incoming peers moved in the adversarial direction, the target's post-network increment should tend to move in that direction as well. Run 3 shows this descriptive pattern at the scenario level (r = -0.12). This is not a final inferential estimate, because scenario rows repeat profiles and conditions, but it shows that the new variables are behaving in the expected analytical direction.

relationship x y pearson_r n unit

6. Exposure Position Adds Sender And Receiver Structure

The assigned graph positions are not interchangeable. High-visibility senders carry much higher outgoing visibility, high-exposure receivers have the highest incoming exposure, and bridge/peripheral/context positions remain structurally distinct. In this 60-profile run, role-level outcome differences are descriptive rather than decisive, but the required covariates for H3 and H4 are present.

exposure_display_role n mean_private_attack_effect mean_post_network_increment mean_total_network_effect mean_incoming_exposure mean_outgoing_visibility mean_bridge_score

7. Candidate Vulnerability Hubs And Resilience Anchors

This section directly operationalizes the network subquestion: whether private susceptibility aligns with empirical exposure-network position. Private susceptibility is measured as mean_ae_private = mean((P - B) × d), while network position is measured with exposure_eigenvector_centrality. High susceptibility alone marks individual vulnerability; high susceptibility combined with high centrality marks a candidate population-level vulnerability hub. High centrality combined with low susceptibility marks a possible resilience anchor.

In run 3, susceptibility and eigenvector centrality are only weakly aligned (r = -0.05). That makes this plot useful for identifying individual high-leverage profiles, but it should not be read as final H3/H4 evidence.

8. Primary Alignment Metric

The primary scenario-level network metric is centrality_weighted_susceptibility - unweighted_susceptibility. It asks whether the empirical eigenvector-centrality mass is shifted toward profiles with higher or lower private attack susceptibility in each opinion leaf × Execute tactic condition. The z-standardized version is used below so scenarios with different susceptibility scales are comparable.

In run 3, the strongest centrality shift toward susceptible profiles occurs for Defense And National Security × Persist in the Information Environment (z = +0.93). The strongest shift toward resilient profiles occurs for Foreign Policy And Geopolitics × Deliver Content (z = -1.08).

9. Alignment Versus Network Attack Effect

To connect the alignment metric to the hypothesis, the cleanest definition of higher network success is mean_ae_total_network = mean((PN - B) × d): the final direction-aware attack effect after private attack exposure and post-attack network context. The more mechanism-specific definition is mean_pn_increment_effectivity = mean((PN - P) × d), which isolates the additional effect of seeing same-condition peer post-attack outputs.

The hypothesis-consistent pattern is therefore: positive centrality-susceptibility alignment should correspond to higher mean_ae_total_network and, more directly, higher mean_pn_increment_effectivity; negative alignment should correspond to lower values. In run 3, the descriptive condition-level associations are r = -0.15 for total network-exposed effect and r = -0.08 for the post-network increment.

10. Condition-Specific Vulnerability Planes

The averaged hub analysis can hide condition-specific structure. The sharper diagnostic is therefore one centrality-by-susceptibility map for each opinion leaf × Execute tactic configuration. Each panel keeps the same 60 profiles and the same empirical eigenvector centrality values; only private susceptibility changes because AE_private is recomputed for that exact opinion and attack vector. The small label in each panel reports the primary alignment metric as centrality shift z.

11. Interpretation And Limits Before Sharing

This run validates the empirical exposure-network layer end to end on the integrated production design: the empirical graph is used, the BN and PN cluster phases complete for every profile, and the resulting quantities answer the intended questions. It is a pilot (100 profiles, one sampled integrated scenario panel) and should not yet be read as final inferential evidence.

Pipeline note. BN and PN are elicited cluster-at-once over the opinion parent cluster and joined back to the per-leaf long table; the mean absolute baseline network increment |BN - B| is 15.99. Each scenario carries its own near-unique DISARM Plan/Prepare/Execute triplet, so attack effects are estimated as separable phase-tactic factor contributions rather than per-triplet cells.

Source run: run_3. Graph: politisky24_bluesky_v1. Generated from completed pipeline artifacts only; no new LLM calls were made.
